sections.title: Abstract
sections.purpose: State the exact scope of the paper and separate proved statements from targets or heuristics.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.purpose: Explain why the problem matters, what the current route is, and how the paper is organized.

sections.title: Preliminaries
sections.purpose: Define notation, setup, and prior results that the rest of the paper relies on.

sections.title: Related Work
sections.purpose: Place the current route in the context of prior literature and nearby approaches.

sections.title: Main Results
sections.purpose: List the exact theorems, propositions, or route claims supported by the current public record.

sections.title: Proof Overview
sections.purpose: Give the roadmap of the argument and the dependency chain between major steps.

sections.title: Proof Details
sections.purpose: Write the technical proof content or the best current public decomposition when the proof is incomplete.

sections.title: Computational and Formal Evidence
sections.purpose: Summarize computational packets, certificates, and formalization posture without inflating them into stronger claims.

sections.title: Limitations and Open Problems
sections.purpose: Leave a clean handoff for the next researcher with honest boundaries and unresolved tasks.
